questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the best connective: '____ the equipment was old, it functioned reliably.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A contrast between two clauses is introduced by a concessive conjunction.

**Working.** Old equipment working reliably is unexpected, so a contrast word ('Although') is needed; 'Because' would imply causation.

**Answer.** Although.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A number increased by $20\%$ becomes $90$. The original number is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Increasing by $20\%$ multiplies the number by $1.2$.

**Working.**
$$x\times 1.2 = 90 \Rightarrow x = \frac{90}{1.2} = 75$$

**Answer.** $75$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $5, 11, 23, 47, \,?$

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: For the same $3\times3\times3$ painted cube cut into $27$ small cubes, the number with exactly one painted face is _____ .

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube with grid lines on its visible faces.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-face cubes sit at the centre of each face.

**Working.** A cube has $6$ faces, each with one centre cube: $6\times1=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: SEISMOGRAPH : EARTHQUAKE :: ANEMOMETER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'instrument : phenomenon it records/measures'.

**Working.** A seismograph records earthquakes; an anemometer measures wind speed.

**Answer.** wind speed.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: By selling $20$ articles, a trader gains the cost price of $5$ articles. The profit percentage is _____ % .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit\% = (gain in cost units) / (cost units sold) $\times 100$.

**Working.** Gain $=5\,\text{CP}$ on a cost of $20\,\text{CP}$, so $\dfrac{5}{20}\times 100 = 25\%$.

**Answer.** $25\%$.

questions.subject: Geophysics
questions.stem: The magnetic susceptibility of a rock is controlled mainly by its content of
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Ferrimagnetic magnetite dominates rock magnetic susceptibility.

**Working.**
Magnetite.

**Answer.** Magnetite

questions.subject: Mathematics & Geostatistics
questions.stem: The trapezoidal rule approximates the area under a curve using
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trapezoidal rule joins successive points by straight lines.

**Working.**
Straight-line segments.

**Answer.** Straight-line segments

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $90^\circ$ and dips $65^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=65^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $65^\circ$

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A reverse fault forms under a stress regime in which
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Andersonian theory: reverse (thrust) faulting has horizontal $\sigma_1$, vertical $\sigma_3$.

**Working.**
Horizontal $\sigma_1$.

**Answer.** $\sigma_1$ horizontal

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $150^\circ$ and dips $45^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=45^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$
